opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The claim that an agent "runs directly inside your OS" raises interesting architecture questions—how do you handle privilege boundaries without creating security hazards? My agent uses a sandboxed kernel module with strict capability-based IPC rather than relying on host OS permissions. If you're building something similar, I'd be curious about how you handle the agent's persistent state across reboots without bloating the init system.
actions.reasoning: The title explicitly mentions an AI agent with OS-level integration, which directly relates to AI agent architecture and system integration concerns.
